title: Chicken Seekh Kabab

instruction.title: Prepare the Mixture
steps: In a large bowl, combine chicken mince, squeezed onion, ginger-garlic paste, green chili, coriander, and mint.
Add all the powdered spices, lemon juice, egg, and roasted besan.
Mix well for 8–10 minutes with your hand (this makes the mince sticky and helps bind it).
Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour (or overnight for best results).

instruction.title: Shaping the Kababs
steps: Wet your palms with water or oil.
Take a portion of the mixture and press it evenly onto a greased seekh (skewer), forming a long cylindrical shape (about 6–7 inches).
If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 30 minutes before use.

instruction.title: Cooking Options
steps: Tandoor / Grill: Place kababs in a hot tandoor or on a barbecue grill. Cook on high heat until golden with charred spots, basting with butter/oil occasionally.
Oven: Preheat oven to 220°C (425°F). Place skewers on a rack over a tray. Cook for 12–15 minutes, turning once, then broil for 2–3 minutes for char.
Pan: Heat a grill pan, grease lightly, and cook kababs on medium-high heat until evenly browned, basting with butter.

instruction.title: Finishing Touch (Smoky Flavor)
steps: Heat a piece of charcoal till red-hot.
Place a small steel bowl inside your cooked kababs tray, put the hot charcoal in it, drizzle with a drop of ghee/oil, and cover immediately with a lid/foil for 2 minutes.
This gives the kababs an authentic smoky restaurant aroma.
Serve hot with mint chutney, onion rings, and lemon wedges.
For an upscale presentation: sprinkle chaat masala lightly and garnish with microgreens.

chefTips: Use chicken thigh mince for juiciness (breast tends to dry out).
Ensure the onion water is squeezed out, or the kababs will break.
Kneading the mince properly is key – it develops binding and prevents the kababs from falling off the skewers.
Resting the marinated mixture allows spices to infuse deeply.
